what_works: Pattern A Megatron-1D shard spec validated: 197 tensors, 0 unresolved
Weight repair: row-parallel on 5 replicated conditioning/embedding layers; per-chip weight 9.5GB -> 8.20GB
Run reaches device execution (mesh (1,8) created, weights loaded, graph compiled)

blocker.error: TT_FATAL Out of Memory: allocate 103809024 B (103MB) DRAM buffer; per-bank free ~26MB, largest free block ~6.9MB (fragmentation)
blocker.buffer_shape_hint: 51,904,512 bf16 elements = total_seq(528) x 98304 (=16*6144); FULL-WIDTH i.e. UNSHARDED/REPLICATED on a single chip
blocker.diagnosis: A large intermediate (~98304 wide) is replicated rather than sharded across the model axis. Weight sharding (iter2->iter3) did NOT change the high-water mark (per-bank allocated ~1.045GB both runs), confirming the limiter is the activation peak, not weight residency. Buffer scales with sequence length.

iterations.cause: SAME DRAM OOM 103MB after weight-shard repair (8.20GB/chip)

recommended_next_steps: runtime_debug: identify the ~98304-wide replicated intermediate (likely a reshape/all-reduce/concat where Shardy sharding propagation drops the model-axis split) and force-shard it or restructure so it stays sharded.
OR reduce_activation_footprint: run at seq_len<=128 (total 144 tokens -> ~28MB buffer) for a reduced-sequence TP MVP pass (Mochi/Wan reduced-res precedent).
dram-space-saving optimization already enabled in the test; fragmentation (largest free block 6.9MB) suggests a packing/allocator angle too.
